query: What is the current state of polling and political dynamics for Zambia's August 2026 presidential election, specifically Hakainde Hichilema's expected vote share? Include recent 2025-2026 polls, opposition coalition strength, whether Edgar Lungu/PF is running (note Lungu died in 2025), and turnout factors.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on Zambia's August 2026 presidential election:

**Hichilema's expected vote share:** Multiple indicators converge on the incumbent being the strong favorite:
- Best scientific poll (Mutombo/UNZA, Oct 2025, n=1,009, ±3.1%): **60%** (58–63%) vs 35% opposition
- Zambia Election Research Network (late 2025): **55%**
- Polymarket implied win probability: **~84.5%** (as of July 11, 2026)
- He won 59% in 2021.

**Lungu/PF status:** Edgar Lungu **died 5 June 2025** in Pretoria — he is NOT running. His death removed the opposition's unifying figure and left the PF (55 National Assembly seats) fractured. A prolonged burial dispute dominated 2025–26 politics.

**Opposition strength:** The main challenger is **Brian Mundubile**, elected Tonse Alliance/PF-Pamodzi candidate on 28 Jan 2026 (running with Makebi Zulu on a National Reconciliation Party ticket due to PF registration disputes). He polls ~35% and has real rally momentum, but the opposition is badly fragmented (26 candidates registered, 14 approved). Provincial arithmetic makes an opposition 50%+1 win difficult — Mundubile would need ~70% in former PF strongholds AND ~55% in battlegrounds simultaneously, a bar even Lungu's incumbent PF missed in 2021.

**Turnout factors:** Analysts project high turnout (>82% cited; ~70% in base models), boosted by 758k new registrants among ~8.7–8.8M registered voters. Dampeners include democratic-backsliding concerns (Cyber Crimes Act arrests, delimitation disputes, Lungu burial grievance) — only ~51% expected a free/fair election.

**Economy** (the central campaign issue) favors Hichilema: inflation down to 6.5%, debt restructured, IMF projecting 4.3% 2026 growth — though cost-of-living and youth unemployment remain grievances. Full report with sourcing and structured data has been delivered.

sources.title: 2026 Zambian general election
sources.snippet: General elections are due to be held in Zambia on 13 August 2026 to elect a president, National Assembly, councillors and council chairs.

sources.title: Opinion poll shows President Hakainde Hichilema headed for victory in 2026
sources.snippet: Mutombo/UNZA poll (Oct 2-15 2025, n=1,009, ±3.1%): Hichilema 60% (58-63%) vs 35% opposition.

sources.title: Zambia election will put Hichilema's economic record to the test (Reuters)
sources.snippet: Hichilema favoured to win but may face strong challenge from Brian Mundubile; inflation 6.5%, IMF 4.3% growth; ZERN poll 55% for HH.

sources.title: Zambia: The Challenges of Maintaining Democratic Progress
sources.snippet: Lungu died June 2025; Tonse Alliance backed away from PF alliance after his death; PF has 55 NA seats; HH won 59% in 2021.

sources.title: Elections 2026: is Zambia's democratic success story beginning to fray?
sources.snippet: 26 candidates registered, 14 approved; opposition fragmentation; Cyber Crimes Act arrest of M'membe; ~8.7M registered voters; Lungu burial controversy.

sources.title: Lungu's death and the perils of a weak opposition in Zambia
sources.snippet: Death of Lungu on 5 June pivotal; without him a fragmented opposition weakens multiparty democracy ahead of 2026.

sources.title: Death and state funeral of Edgar Lungu
sources.snippet: Lungu died 5 June 2025 in Pretoria, age 68; burial dispute; SA court ruled 8 Aug 2025 for Zambian government repatriation.

sources.title: Mundubile elected Tonse Alliance presidential candidate
sources.snippet: Mundubile elected Tonse Alliance candidate 28 Jan 2026, defeating Danny Pule 129-34.

sources.snippet: Tonse-PF Pamodzi Alliance candidate running with Makebi Zulu on National Reconciliation Party ticket.

sources.snippet: Provincial arithmetic: Mundubile needs ~70% in former PF strongholds and ~55% in battlegrounds simultaneously to reach 50%+1.

sources.title: HH wins 2026 presidential opinion poll among top six candidates
sources.snippet: Zambian Post Facebook straw poll (~67,600): HH 55%, Mundubile 35%, Zulu 6%.

sources.title: Zambia Presidential Election Winner (Polymarket)
sources.snippet: Hichilema implied win probability ~84.5% on 11 July 2026.
